title: Fluffy Cozy Mexican Chiles Rellenos
description: Cultured, fluffy, and cozy Mexican dish featuring roasted poblano peppers stuffed with a sweet and savory filling, then battered and fried to perfection. Served with a side of creamy tomato sauce.
instructions: Roast the poblano peppers over an open flame or in the oven at 400°F (200°C) for 10-12 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.
Peel the skin off the peppers and cut a slit down one side of each pepper to create a pocket.
In a blender or food processor, combine the corn kernels, black beans, onion, garlic, cilantro, vanilla extract, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Blend until smooth.
Stuff each pepper with the corn and bean mixture, dividing it evenly among the peppers.
In a shallow dish, mix together the flour, cornstarch, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Dip each stuffed pepper into the flour mixture, coating it evenly, then dip it into the buttermilk, and finally coat it in the flour mixture again.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the coated peppers and fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the peppers from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat. Fry the peppers until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Drain the peppers on paper towels and serve with a side of creamy tomato sauce and a sprinkle of cilantro.
To make the tomato sauce, combine the tomato sauce, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper in a blender or food processor. Blend until smooth.
To make the creamy sauce, combine the sour cream, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper in a blender or food processor. Blend until smooth.
To assemble the dish, place a stuffed and fried pepper on a plate, spoon some creamy tomato sauce over the top, and sprinkle with chopped cilantro and a pinch of cayenne pepper.
Garnish with additional chopped cilantro and a sprinkle of cayenne pepper, if desired.
